prompt:
A Field Service Membership & Data Governance system with main features including secure account access, member lifecycle operations, searchable records, auditable activity tracking, offline data standardization, and address-based service eligibility checks. The interface is built with HTMX to deliver fast, form-driven workflows without a heavy single-page app: staff sign in with a locally verified username and password, then land on a role-based home view for Member Desk, Dispatch, or Admin tasks. Member Desk users can create and maintain member profiles with status, tags, and organization links, and run guided workflows for join, renew, upgrade, downgrade, deactivate, or cancel; each step gives immediate inline validation feedback and a timeline view that explains what changed, by whom, and when (MM/DD/YYYY, 12-hour time). Dispatch users manage an address book for members and sites, select a location via manual pin placement on an offline floorplan-style grid and/or coordinate entry, and validate whether a request is inside supported service areas defined by administrative region rules or a maximum dispatch radius (default 25 miles). Search is available from every screen with full-text matching, keyword highlighting, filters by tags/categories and time range, and configurable “trending searches” plus no-result recommendations that suggest nearby tags or recent members to reduce dead ends.

The backend uses Flask to expose REST-style endpoints consumed by HTMX partial requests, enforcing server-side validation and consistent state transitions. SQLite provides local persistence with transactional guarantees for member records, address book entries, service areas, audit logs, and cleansing templates; write operations are atomic, and conflicting edits are rejected using optimistic concurrency with a 60-second edit lock warning. Authentication is strictly offline: passwords are hashed with a strong adaptive hash, accounts lock for 15 minutes after 5 failed attempts, and admins can freeze or deactivate accounts, manage trusted devices per user (limit 5 devices), and require re-authentication for sensitive actions after 30 minutes of inactivity. Operational auditing is comprehensive: logins, permission changes, all create/read/update/delete events, and exports are recorded with timestamps and the acting user, and administrators can search logs, flag anomalies (for example, more than 50 record reads in 10 minutes), and monitor local SLA targets such as “member search returns results within 2 seconds for 50,000 records” with alerts shown in an on-screen console. The data cleansing pipeline runs fully offline on imported CSV files, supporting field mapping, missing-value rules, deduplication using configurable match thresholds, outlier flags, standardized USD and imperial units, normalized date-time formats, and place-name standardization using a locally maintained reference table; templates are reusable and versioned, with sensitive fields masked in the UI and encrypted at rest within the SQLite file using an application-managed encryption key stored on the host machine.
